title: Diagnostics + Data-Sourcing Dossier for the Two-Track Co-Response SAE-Grouping Study
summary: Executor-ready dossier de-risking the C3 absorption spine and pinning every data/infra build. WP1: the Chanin 2409.14507 absorption diagnostic verified against the lasr-spelling/sae-spelling code (parent=encoder-cosine-with-LR-probe + k-sparse splits; absorber=largest-negative integrated-gradients ablation on the first-letter logit-diff m=g[y]-mean(g[incorrect]); thresholds probe_cos_sim_threshold=0.025 on the DECODER (W_dec) direction, ablation_delta_threshold=1.0, f1_jump_threshold=0.03, EPS=1e-8, 200-FN cap, IG steps=6, topk=10; absorption_rate=num_absorptions/lr_probe_true_positives; layers 0-17 only), PLUS a strictly non-circular FORM-FREE version the paper supplies in Appendix A.13 and SAEBench implements as absorption_fraction (latent l absorbs iff tau_c < (a_hat_l . d_p)/(a . d_p), a_hat_l = enc_act*W_dec, works at all layers, needs only a parent-concept LR probe d_p disjoint from the clustering data). WP2: absorption is empirically documented ONLY on first-letter spelling (LessWrong toy-models post, SAEBench eval id 'absorption_first_letter', Matryoshka/H-SAE only mitigate via the spelling metric) so Testbed-2 is both a generality test and a novel empirical test; numeric-quantity hierarchy recommended primary (taxonomic country alt) with concrete non-triviality gates (parent recall>=0.60; >=1 absorber with Jaccard<0.10, sub-context precision>=0.70, hole-coverage gain>=0.05 with bootstrap CI excluding 0) and an honest-null fallback (scope to spelling, route generality via C1). WP3: pinned HF datasets (s-nlp/paradetox en_toxic_comment/en_neutral_comment 19,744 rows openrail++; google/civil_comments text+7 floats 1.8M/97k/97k CC0 414.95MB needs subsample; tasksource/counterfactually-augmented-imdb Text/Sentiment no pair-ids license unknown -> acmi-lab GitHub for pairing; CEBaB/CEBaB full aspect-majority schema, license NOT on card=TODO; LabHC/bias_in_bios hard_text/profession-int64-0-27/gender 257k/39.6k/99.1k 266MB MIT with full alphabetical profession map). WP4: OpenRouter minimal-pair generation prompts + independent LLM-judge rubric + verified June-2026 prices (generator gemini-3.1-flash-lite $0.25/$1.50 or deepseek-v4-flash $0.09/$0.18; judge claude-haiku-4.5 $1.00/$5.00) totalling ~$5.9-7.6 for ~5,000 pairs with a hard $10 stop. WP5: sae-spelling get_alpha_tokens (convert_tokens_to_string then strip one leading space then all-alpha) and prompt template '{word} has the first letter:' (pos -6); pile-uncopyrighted pinned rev 3be9033 (2023-08-31, non-streaming); SAE.from_pretrained(release='gemma-scope-2b-pt-res-canonical', sae_id='layer_12/width_16k/canonical') d_model=2304 + the 65k variant; CRITICAL model-diffing finding: NO gemma-scope-2b-it SAE exists (Google IT residual SAEs only for 9B) -> use the SHARED pt SAE on both gemma-2-2b and gemma-2-2b-it activations; both gated, use unsloth/gemma-2-2b(-it) mirrors.
answer:
See research_report.md for the full 5-work-package report. Executive summary of every pinned fact follows, each with citations.

=== WP1: CHANIN 2409.14507 ABSORPTION DIAGNOSTIC (verified vs sae-spelling code) ===
SAE config: base gemma-2-2b, Gemma Scope residual-stream SAEs width 16k AND 65k, layers 0-17 ONLY (metric breaks past layer 18 because spelling info moves to the prediction token); cross-validated on Qwen2-0.5B and Llama-3.2-1B layers 0-8 [1].
Task/prompt: first-letter ICL, template 'tartan has the first letter: T / ...'; repo constant VERBOSE_FIRST_LETTER_TEMPLATE='{word} has the first letter:' with token pos -6; the absorption calculator reads at word_token_pos=-2 [1,3,5].
Step A parent latent: train an LR probe (k-sparse multi-probe, L1 decay 0.01) on residuals; parent = SAE latent whose ENCODER direction has max cosine with the probe (26 first-letter latents); k=1 sparse probing gives similar results [1,4].
Step B feature-split count: k-sparse probing KS=(1..15,20,50); a split = F1 jump from k=n to k=n+1 exceeding f1_jump_threshold=0.03 (range 0.01-0.05) [1,3].
Step C false-negatives: tokens the LR probe gets right but ALL k split latents miss; cap 200 per letter (random subsample) [1].
Step D ablation: integrated-gradients ablation (ig_interpolation_steps=6, ig_batch_size=6) over latents, SAE inserted WITH error term; metric m=g[y]-(1/(|L|-1))*sum_{l!=y} g[l] (correct-letter logit minus mean incorrect-letter logit); take topk_feats=10 by most-negative IG [1,3].
Step E _is_absorption (verbatim code logic): (1) every main-latent activation < EPS=1e-8; (2) top ablation score <= 0; (3) |top IG| - |2nd IG| >= ablation_delta_threshold=1.0 (range 0.5-3.0); (4) top latent's DECODER-direction cosine with probe >= probe_cos_sim_threshold=0.025 (range 0-0.05) [1,3]. CRITICAL: parent ID uses ENCODER cosine, but the absorber alignment check uses the DECODER: code is cos_sims=cosine_similarity(probe_dir, sae.W_dec, dim=-1) [3].
Step F rate: absorption_rate = num_absorptions / lr_probe_true_positives [1].
Worked example / Tier-0 pilot ground truth: layer 3, width 16k, L0=59 SAE; 'starts with S' main latent 6510 (F1~0.81) misses _short; absorber 1085 ('short', decoder-probe cosine 0.12) fires instead with ~5x magnitude and dominant IG effect; projection-removal kills it. Feature split for L: latent 7112 (lowercase l) + 7657 (uppercase L) [1].
SAEBench (2503.09532) re-implements as eval id 'absorption_first_letter' (spelling-only) and reports BOTH mean_full_absorption_score (strict) and mean_absorption_fraction_score (relaxed/projection-based) plus mean_num_split_features [6,7].

FORM-FREE version (paper's own Appendix A.13; SAEBench's absorption_fraction): replace IG ablation with a probe-projection-in-reconstruction test. Project each firing latent's reconstruction contribution a_hat_l = (enc activation of l)*(W_dec[l]) and the input a onto the parent LR-probe direction d_p; latent l is an absorber iff tau_c < (a_hat_l . d_p)/(a . d_p); multi-absorber sum over N largest; relax 'main fully off' to 'fires weakly' for partial absorption [1]. This works at ALL layers and replaces the 'concept logit' (undefined when there is no single output token) with the probe-direction projection of the residual. Supervision needed = a parent-concept label set to train d_p, drawn from data DISJOINT from the clustering counterfactual pairs, plus the frozen W_dec - independent of the SAE latents being grouped [1].
NON-CIRCULARITY: the diagnostic only SCORES already-formed specialization (KG) edges and validates the Tier-0 pilot; units are formed by content-response correlation (C-track) and anchored greedy set-cover on highest-recall latents (K-track), never by the absorption metric; the by-construction risk (holes ~ parent false-negatives) is controlled by a matched random-latent null with bootstrap CI excluding 0 [1].

=== WP2: ABSORPTION BEYOND SPELLING? + TESTBED-2 ===
No peer-reviewed source demonstrates absorption on a NON-spelling token hierarchy: Chanin 2409.14507 empirics are first-letter only [1]; the LessWrong toy-models post says only spelling has real-model support, semantic 'X is Y' cases are illustrative and the fix was untested on non-toy SAEs [9]; SAEBench's only absorption eval is 'absorption_first_letter' [7]; Feature Hedging 2505.11756 adds a correlated-feature mode (absorption worse at WIDER SAEs, hedging worse at NARROWER) on the same spelling-type setup [8]; Matryoshka (2503.17547) and H-SAE (2506.01197) only MITIGATE absorption measured by the spelling metric [10,11]; Dense SAE Latents (2506.15679) shows numeric/year features exist in SAEs (supports a numeric parent's plausibility) but not absorption [12]. => Testbed-2 is simultaneously a generality test AND a novel empirical test; do not fabricate evidence.
Design: PRIMARY = numeric-quantity hierarchy (general 'numeric token' parent; absorbers for 4-digit years/percentages/dates; source single-token numerics from pile-uncopyrighted; content-flip = number<->non-number in matched templates; sub-context label = year/percentage/date/other). ALTERNATIVE = taxonomic 'is-a-country' (parent country-name; absorbers per continent; country->continent labels). Numeric recommended primary: number-ness is dense (the dense-parent/sparse-child condition) and labels are objective single-token (clean degenerate-construction guard); taxonomic entities are often multi-token.
Non-triviality go/no-go (all required): (i) parent latent content-response recall >= 0.60 (spelling parent F1~0.81 justifies a 0.60 floor); (ii) >=1 (target >=2) absorber with firing Jaccard < 0.10 vs parent AND sub-context content-response precision >= 0.70 AND marginal hole-coverage gain >= 0.05 with bootstrap 95% CI excluding 0. Else: report absorption is spelling-specific, scope the C3 title to spelling-type hierarchical absorption, route generality through C1 (a clean negative is publishable, not a failure).

=== WP3: DATASETS (HF id | splits(rows) | needed cols(dtype) | size | license | gotcha) ===
ParaDetox: s-nlp/paradetox | train 19,744 | en_toxic_comment(str), en_neutral_comment(str) | 2.11MB | openrail++ (derived Jigsaw/Toloka) | ~1.66 neutral paraphrases per toxic (11,939 toxic -> 19,766 paraphrases) [13].
civil_comments: google/civil_comments | train 1,804,874 / validation 97,320 / test 97,320 | text(str) + toxicity,severe_toxicity,obscene,threat,insult,identity_attack,sexual_explicit (all float32) | 414.95MB (>300MB) | CC0 1.0 | SUBSAMPLE (stream+filter, cap ~150k); the 5 sub-attr floats = independent sub-context labels [14].
CAD-IMDB: tasksource/counterfactually-augmented-imdb | train 1,710 / validation 245 / test 488 | Text(str), Sentiment(2 cls) | 2.26MB | UNKNOWN | NO pair-ids in this mirror -> use acmi-lab/counterfactually-augmented-data (GitHub) for paired original<->revised, verify row alignment [17].
CEBaB: CEBaB/CEBaB | train_inclusive 11,700 / train_exclusive 1,760 / train_observational 1,760 / validation 1,670 / test 1,690 | id, original_id, edit_id, is_original(bool), edit_goal(4cls), edit_type(5cls), edit_worker, description(review), review_majority(5cls), food/service/ambiance/noise_aspect_majority (each 4cls: Positive/Negative/unknown/'') | small | LICENSE NOT DECLARED on HF card (gated:false) = TODO confirm via GitHub/paper | drop ''/'no majority' rows; pair via original_id+edit_id; food+service = one nested family, aspect majorities = independent sub-contexts [16].
bias_in_bios: LabHC/bias_in_bios | train 257,000 / dev 39,600 / test 99,100 | hard_text(str), profession(int64 0-27), gender(int64 0=M/1=F) | 266MB (<=300MB) | MIT | profession int->name map is ALPHABETICAL: 0 accountant,1 architect,2 attorney,3 chiropractor,4 comedian,5 composer,6 dentist,7 dietitian,8 dj,9 filmmaker,10 interior_designer,11 journalist,12 model,13 nurse,14 painter,15 paralegal,16 pastor,17 personal_trainer,18 photographer,19 physician,20 poet,21 professor,22 psychologist,23 rapper,24 software_engineer,25 surgeon,26 teacher,27 yoga_teacher; pre-registered boundary-null testbed [15].

=== WP4: LLM MINIMAL-PAIR GEN + JUDGE (OpenRouter, <$10) ===
Human corpora used where available; LLM generation only for first-letter/numeric/taxonomic. Output pair-record schema {pair_id, concept, sub_context_label, x_off, x_on, target_token, surface_variant, is_surface_pair}. Generation prompts: (a) first-letter single-token-swap in fixed template; (b) numeric token<->non-numeric/format swap; (c) country<->non-country entity swap; (d) surface-flip paraphrase keeping concept token (is_surface_pair=true). Judge rubric returns {content_flipped, surface_preserved, fluent, confidence, justification}; pass = content_flipped AND surface_preserved AND fluent; report pass rate (target >=0.85); discard fails; USE A DIFFERENT JUDGE MODEL than the generator.
Verified June-2026 OpenRouter prices ($/M in, $/M out): generator google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite (0.25, 1.50) or cheapest deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash (0.09, 0.18); judge anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 (1.00, 5.00) [independent family], cheap alt google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite (0.25,1.50), strong-but-tight google/gemini-3.5-flash (1.50, 9.00) [18]. Volume ~5,000 pair records (n_min=150 positive x ~14 sub-contexts + surface variants). Arithmetic (gen 500 in/200 out; judge 350 in/150 out): gen gemini-flash-lite=$2.13 (deepseek=$0.41); judge haiku-4.5=$5.50 (gemini-flash-lite=$1.56; gemini-3.5-flash=$9.38 avoid). Recommended total ~$7.6 (gemini-flash-lite gen + haiku-4.5 judge); budget ~$5.9 (deepseek gen + haiku judge); cheapest ~$2.0. Hard stop at $10: drop to numeric+3 letters or batch 10 pairs/judge call. Any activation-space edit must use an independent held-out diff-of-means on disjoint data, never the SAE latents being grouped.

=== WP5: FIRST-LETTER + SAE INFRA ===
sae-spelling get_alpha_tokens(tokenizer, allow_leading_space=True, replace_special_chars=True): tokens normalized via tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_string([token]) (turns Gemma's leading underscore/space-piece into ' '), then ONE leading space stripped, then require all chars in a-zA-Z; casing preserved [4]. Prompt: VERBOSE_FIRST_LETTER_TEMPLATE='{word} has the first letter:' pos -6; first_letter('cat')->' C' [5]. Entry point sae_spelling.experiments.feature_absorption -> FeatureAbsorptionCalculator.calculate_absorption_sampled(sae, words, probe_dir, metric_fn, main_feature_ids, max_ablation_samples) [3]. Pilot ground-truth absorbers: S->'short' (1085 vs 6510), L-> 7112 + 7657 [1].
pile-uncopyrighted: monology/pile-uncopyrighted, PIN rev 3be9033 (2023-08-31 'Upload to LFS'; full hash begins 3be90335..., TODO record 40-char hash at build time); use NON-streaming load (documented streaming issue) [20].
Gemma Scope SAE: SAE.from_pretrained(release='gemma-scope-2b-pt-res-canonical', sae_id='layer_12/width_16k/canonical'); underlying google/gemma-scope-2b-pt-res, model gemma-2-2b, d_model=2304, canonical=avg L0 closest to 100; 65k variant sae_id='layer_12/width_65k/canonical' (both present) [18,19]. hook_name='blocks.12.hook_resid_post', W_dec shape [16384,2304].
MODEL-DIFFING CRITICAL: NO gemma-scope-2b-it SAE exists anywhere in the SAELens registry; Google IT residual SAEs exist ONLY for 9B (gemma-scope-9b-it-res / -canonical, model gemma-2-9b-it); 'Gemma Scope 2' covers the Gemma 3 family (270M/1B/4B/12B/27B), not 2B; jbloom/Gemma-2b-IT-Residual-Stream-SAEs is for Gemma-1 2B-it, incompatible. => For model-diffing, apply the SAME frozen pt SAE (gemma-scope-2b-pt-res-canonical layer_12/width_16k) to activations from BOTH gemma-2-2b and gemma-2-2b-it and diff cluster-level statistics (shared dictionary); only fallback for a paired it-SAE is switching the whole study to gemma-2-9b + gemma-scope-9b-{pt,it}-res (different model/width) [18].
Models gemma-2-2b + gemma-2-2b-it are GATED on HF; non-gated mirrors unsloth/gemma-2-2b and unsloth/gemma-2-2b-it (tokenizer vocab 256000) [21].

sources.title: A is for Absorption: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders (Chanin et al., NeurIPS 2024) - HTML
sources.summary: Primary source for the absorption diagnostic: parent=encoder-cosine-with-LR-probe + k-sparse splits; absorber=largest-negative IG ablation on first-letter logit-diff m; thresholds cos>=0.025 (decoder, Fig 6b), ablation gap>=1.0, f1-jump 0.03, 200-FN cap; absorption_rate=num_absorptions/lr_probe_true_positives; layers 0-17; worked S/L examples (6510/1085, 7112/7657); Appendix A.13 form-free probe-projection variant tau_c<(a_hat_l.d_p)/(a.d_p).

sources.title: A is for Absorption (arXiv abstract / NeurIPS 2024 listing)
sources.summary: Abstract and venue confirmation; introduces the absorption metric validated on hundreds of LLM SAEs.

sources.title: lasr-spelling/sae-spelling FeatureAbsorptionCalculator (authoritative code)
sources.summary: Verifies probe_cos_sim_threshold=0.025 on sae.W_dec (decoder), ablation_delta_threshold=1.0, word_token_pos=-2, ig_interpolation_steps=6, ig_batch_size=6, topk_feats=10, EPS=1e-8, and the exact _is_absorption 4-condition logic + calculate_absorption_sampled signature.

sources.title: lasr-spelling/sae-spelling vocab.py get_alpha_tokens
sources.summary: get_alpha_tokens recipe: convert_tokens_to_string normalization, strip one leading space, require all chars in a-zA-Z; LETTERS/LETTERS_UPPER; k-sparse LR probe L1 decay 0.01.

sources.title: lasr-spelling/sae-spelling prompting.py
sources.summary: VERBOSE_FIRST_LETTER_TEMPLATE='{word} has the first letter:', VERBOSE_FIRST_LETTER_TOKEN_POS=-6, first_letter()/first_letter_formatter() definitions and SpellingPrompt/ICL construction.

sources.title: SAEBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Sparse Autoencoders (Karvonen, Rager et al.)
sources.summary: SAEBench re-implements absorption via the first-letter task: LR probe ground-truth direction + k-sparse probing to find main latents, then absorption where probe is right but main latents fail.

sources.title: SAEBench absorption eval output schema (adamkarvonen/SAEBench)
sources.summary: EVAL_TYPE_ID_ABSORPTION='absorption_first_letter' (spelling-only); reports mean_absorption_fraction_score (relaxed/projection) AND mean_full_absorption_score (strict) AND mean_num_split_features, with per-letter full_absorption_rate/num_full_absorption.

sources.title: Feature Hedging: Correlated Features Break Narrow Sparse Autoencoders (Chanin, Dulka, Garriga-Alonso)
sources.summary: Second correlated-feature failure mode; absorption worse at WIDER SAEs while hedging worse at NARROWER; suspects hedging explains SAE underperformance vs supervised baselines; proposes improved matryoshka SAEs.

sources.title: Toy Models of Feature Absorption in SAEs (LessWrong)
sources.summary: Confirms only the spelling hierarchy has empirical real-model support; semantic 'X is Y' (dogs/animals) examples are illustrative; tied-weight fix untested on non-toy SAEs.

sources.title: Learning Multi-Level Features with Matryoshka Sparse Autoencoders (Bussmann et al.)
sources.summary: Matryoshka SAEs reduce feature absorption (measured on Gemma-2-2B via the spelling-type metric) at a small reconstruction tradeoff; a mitigation, not a new non-spelling absorption demonstration.

sources.title: Incorporating Hierarchical Semantics in Sparse Autoencoder Architectures (Muchane, Richardson, ... Veitch)
sources.summary: H-SAE architecture; standard SAEs show higher absorption (more splitting), H-SAE more coherent; absorption still measured via the spelling task.

sources.title: Dense SAE Latents Are Features, Not Bugs (Sun, Stolfo, ... Tegmark)
sources.summary: Numeric/temporal features (e.g. years) ARE represented in SAEs, often as dense latents; supports plausibility of a numeric parent latent but does not demonstrate absorption on a numeric hierarchy.

sources.title: s-nlp/paradetox (HuggingFace)
sources.summary: Columns en_toxic_comment / en_neutral_comment; single train split 19,744 rows; 2.11MB; license openrail++; ~1.66 neutral paraphrases per toxic (11,939 toxic -> 19,766 paraphrases).

sources.title: google/civil_comments (HuggingFace)
sources.summary: text(str) + 7 float32 sub-attributes; train 1,804,874 / validation 97,320 / test 97,320; 414.95MB download (>300MB so subsample); license CC0 1.0.

sources.title: LabHC/bias_in_bios (HuggingFace)
sources.summary: hard_text(str), profession(int64 0-27), gender(int64 0=M/1=F); train 257,000 / dev 39,600 / test 99,100; 266MB; MIT; full alphabetical profession int->name map captured.

sources.title: CEBaB/CEBaB (HuggingFace)
sources.summary: Full aspect-majority schema (id, original_id, edit_id, is_original, edit_goal[4], edit_type[5], edit_worker, description, review_majority[5], food/service/ambiance/noise_aspect_majority[4]); splits train_inclusive/exclusive/observational + validation/test; gated:false; LICENSE NOT declared on the card (TODO).

sources.title: tasksource/counterfactually-augmented-imdb (HuggingFace)
sources.summary: Text(str) + Sentiment(2 cls); train 1,710 / validation 245 / test 488; 2.26MB; license 'unknown'; NO pairing ids -> use acmi-lab/counterfactually-augmented-data GitHub for paired original<->revised.

sources.title: SAELens pretrained_saes.yaml registry (decoderesearch/SAELens, formerly jbloomAus)
sources.summary: Confirms gemma-scope-2b-pt-res-canonical with layer_12/width_16k/canonical AND layer_12/width_65k/canonical; gemma-scope-9b-it-res / -canonical exist (model gemma-2-9b-it) but NO gemma-scope-2b-it release of any kind; 'Gemma Scope 2' targets Gemma 3 family not 2B.

sources.title: google/gemma-scope-2b-pt-res + SAELens docs (from_pretrained example)
sources.summary: SAE.from_pretrained(release='gemma-scope-2b-pt-res-canonical', sae_id='layer_12/width_16k/canonical', device=...); gemma-2-2b d_model=2304; canonical = average L0 closest to 100.

sources.title: monology/pile-uncopyrighted commit history (HuggingFace)
sources.summary: Latest main commit 3be9033 ('Upload to LFS', 2023-08-31); recent 3f950f4 (README, 08-30), 743bf92 (initial, 08-30); pin this revision; prefer non-streaming load.

sources.title: unsloth/gemma-2-2b and unsloth/gemma-2-2b-it (non-gated mirrors)
sources.summary: Google gemma-2-2b / gemma-2-2b-it are gated (require auth); unsloth mirrors are non-gated, architecture-identical fallbacks; tokenizer vocab 256000.

follow_up_questions: CEBaB declares no license on its HuggingFace card (gated:false) - what are the redistribution terms in the CEBaB GitHub repo / NeurIPS-2022 paper, and is research-only use sufficient for the study's release?

For model-diffing, does applying the single frozen gemma-scope-2b-pt-res SAE to gemma-2-2b-it activations (shared-dictionary diffing) give a meaningful enough signal, or must the study migrate to gemma-2-9b + the paired gemma-scope-9b-{pt,it}-res SAEs (different model/width/layer) to use a genuinely paired IT SAE?

Empirically, does a high-recall general 'numeric token' parent latent (recall >= 0.60) with disjoint-support year/percentage/date absorbers actually exist in Gemma Scope gemma-2-2b layer_12 SAEs - i.e. will Testbed-2's non-triviality gate pass, or will C3 generality have to be routed through C1 with a spelling-only scope?
